query: If a 4-hour battery earns revenues from arbitrage only in ERCOT West hub over 15 years, what should the $/MW capex be to earn 13% IRR? Assume roundtrip efficiency is 81%, battery degradation cost is $25/MWh and state of charge is limited to a range of 10% to 90%, prices from 2010 to 2024 represent the 15-year window
final_answer:
## Final Answer: Maximum Capex for 13% IRR — 4-Hour Battery, ERCOT West Hub

### **$278,453/MW** (or **$69.61/kWh** for a 4-hour battery)

---

### Methodology

| Parameter | Value | Source |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| **Price data** | ERCOT West Hub hourly LMPs | `ercot_db`, table `ercot_lmp` |
| **Period** | 2011–2024 (14 full years; year 15 = average of all years) | — |
| **Battery duration** | 4 hours | User-specified |
| **Roundtrip efficiency** | 81% | User-specified |
| **Degradation cost** | $25/MWh | User-specified |
| **SOC range** | 10%–90% | User-specified |
| **Optimization** | Perfect-foresight energy arbitrage (battery_revenue_optimization tool) | — |

### Annual Net Revenues (after degradation + charging costs)

| Year | Net Revenue ($/MW) | Year | Net Revenue ($/MW) |
|------|--------------------:|------|--------------------:|
| 2011 | $87,813 | 2018 | $37,198 |
| 2012 | $19,696 | 2019 | $101,576 |
| 2013 | $14,684 | 2020 | $22,141 |
| 2014 | $23,073 | 2021 | $94,283 |
| 2015 | $12,566 | 2022 | $90,416 |
| 2016 | $9,785 | 2023 | $150,843 |
| 2017 | $8,324 | 2024 | $40,017 |
| — | — | **Avg (Year 15)** | **$50,887** |

**Average annual arbitrage revenue: $50,887/MW/yr**

### Capex Derivation

At a 13% IRR, the NPV of the 15-year revenue stream equals zero, so:

> **Max Capex = Σ (Revenueₜ / (1.13)^t) for t = 1…15 = $278,453/MW**

Verified by computing the IRR at this capex — it returns exactly **13.00%**.

### Key Caveats
- Uses **perfect-foresight** (upper-bound) arbitrage — real-world revenues will be lower.
- 2010 data was unavailable (only December existed), so years 2011–2024 were used with the 14-year average repeated as the 15th year.
- No capacity payments, ancillary services, or O&M costs are included — this is **arbitrage revenue only**.

]

target_irr = 0.13

# At the IRR, NPV = 0:
# -Capex + sum(CF_t / (1+IRR)^t) = 0
# Capex = sum(CF_t / (1+IRR)^t)

# Maximum capex = NPV of cash flows at 13% discount rate
npv = sum(cf / (1 + target_irr)**t for t, cf in enumerate(revenues_15yr, 1))

print("=" * 70)

### Capex Derivation

At a 13% IRR, the NPV of the 15-year revenue stream equals zero, so:
